item.title: 1990's made Fender Japan '70 Reissue Precision Bass PB70-US Olympic White Finish Crafted in Japan — $843
item.description:
$843 · Very Good · Finest Quality Japan Made Guitars

We have this guitar in stock.
We can ship this guitar within 1 business day after payment is received.If there is any problem with the shipment, you will receive a full refund. Please buy with confidence.

Attention U.S.A. Buyers

Due to recent U.S. trade policies, additional import duties (Trump-era tariffs) may be applied to certain products.
Please rest assured that all applicable import duties will be covered by our shop, so you will not need to pay any additional fees.
You can purchase with confidence.
We handle all customs and shipping professionally to ensure a smooth delivery.
-----------------------------------------------------------
1990's Fender Japan '70 Reissue Precision Bass PB70-US Olympic White Crafted in Japan

You're looking at a Fender Japan '70 Reissue Precision Bass PB70-US in very good playing condition with an Olympic White finish.

This bass features a comfortable 40mm nut width with a C-shaped neck and delivers the clear, powerful tone that the PB70 series is well known for.
One of the most remarkable features of this instrument is its outstanding cosmetic condition.

Although this is a rare 1990s model, it has been exceptionally well preserved.
Many PB70 basses from this period have sticker marks, discoloration, or heavy signs of use.
This one has no sticker marks at all, making it an especially hard-to-find example.

Features:
(1) Alder body with beautiful Olympic White finish
(2) Comfortable 40mm nut width with C-shaped neck
(3) Rare 1990s Crafted in Japan model in excellent condition
(4) Beautiful dark rosewood fingerboard
(5) Classic vintage Precision Bass tone
Please check the video for detailed condition.
For safe international shipping, I will remove the neck from the body before packing.

Please don't worry about shipping damage.
If you have any questions, please feel free to ask.

・Finish　　 Olympic White
・Body　　 Alder
・Finger Board　　 Rosewood
・Weight　　 4.0kg (8.80 pounds)
・Serial Number　　 O038680 (1997–2000)
・Brand　　 Fender Japan
・Manufacture Country　　 Japan
・Nut Width　　 40mm
・Pickups　　 Original
・Case　　 No Case
・Neck　　 Straight
・Tuner　　 Working Well
・Truss Rod　　 Working Well
・Electrics　　 Working Well
・Pickups　　 Working Well
-------------------------------------------------------------
Condition Rating (85/100)
There are a few small dents on the sides of the body.
Other than that, there are no noticeable major scratches or damage.
Over the years, the finish has naturally aged to a beautiful cream-white color, giving the bass an attractive vintage appearance.

The neck is straight, and the truss rod works properly.
The frets are in very good condition with approximately 80% remaining.

(No fret buzzing)
Sounds and plays great with no issues.

Please check the photos for details.
Please check the video for sound samples and detailed condition.

I will pack the bass very carefully to ensure safe shipping.
Please don't worry about shipping damage.

Please check my feedback from previous buyers.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

item.title: Fender Precision Bass PB62-53 1999-2002 - Vintage White — $1,100
item.description:
$1,100 · Very Good · Alien Rabbit Guitar Workshop

1999–2002 Fender Japan Precision Bass.


Classic ’62-style specifications, finished in Vintage White with a red tortoise pickguard. The overall look has a great old-school Fender vibe.

The Precision Bass is one of the most straightforward and practical bass designs out there, with a thick sound and solid low end. It works great for Rock, Pop, Funk, Indie, and many other styles.

This bass is equipped with the original PB-Vintage pickup, delivering a traditional Precision Bass tone with clear attack, solid mids, and a strong low-end presence. It sits easily in the mix, whether for recording or live performance.

Made in Japan with reliable craftsmanship and a comfortable playing feel. If you are looking for a classic-looking, highly practical Precision Bass, this one is definitely worth checking out.


  • Year: 1999–2002
  • Origin: Japan
  • Condition: Used
  • Color: VWH
  • Body: Basswood
  • Neck: Maple
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Radius: 7.25”
  • Pickup: PB-Vintage x1

item.title: 【used】G&L / L-2000 Tribute Series BLK 4.150kg #151206533【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $894
item.description:
$894 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

G&L is known as a brand founded by Leo Fender, following Fender and Music Man.

It is favored by many bassists, including Tom Hamilton (Aerosmith) and Murphy Karges (Sugar Ray). (Titles omitted)

This model is the L-2000 Tribute Series, G&L's flagship model.

While produced in Indonesia, the Tribute Series inherits the design philosophy of the USA model, achieving high cost performance.

The body is made of ash, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple.

This wood configuration supports the L-2000's characteristic sound, with a crisp attack and powerful low end.

It is equipped with two large original humbucking pickups.

Furthermore, by combining it with a 2-band EQ preamp, it can handle a wide range of tones, from thick and powerful sounds to bright and clear tones.

The three mini-switches are also a characteristic feature of the L-2000.

This bass offers a wide range of sound shaping possibilities with its freely controllable pickup selection, pickup wiring (series/tap/parallel), and preamp switching (treble boost/active/passive).

It combines a powerful, punchy sound with the flexibility offered by its diverse controls.

This bass is highly practical and suitable for any genre.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck has been adjusted with shims due to the bridge saddle dropping to its limit. It is playable without issues at 2.5mm for the 4th string and 2.0mm for the 1st string.

There are some minor scratches on the body and paintwork.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were found.

Controls: Master Volume, Treble, Bass, Pickup Selector Switch, Pickup Wiring Switch (Series/Tap/Parallel), Preamp Selector Switch (Treble Boost/Active/Passive)
Nut Width: 43.0mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: INDONESIA

During setup, we use our original GIB BASIC / Standard Bass Strings.

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.

These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Gig bag

Text: Mizune Ishikawa

Inspection 담당: Oshima

Product Photos: Tei

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for lifetime free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

The warranty period is 3 months for used products and 1 year for new products.

item.title: 【used】HEX Guitars / NB300 Ivory 2.760kg #HJ1202048【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $818
item.description:
$818 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

HEX Guitars is a South Korea-based brand specializing in entry-level to mid-range guitars.

This model is the NB300, a headless bass guitar with a 30-inch short scale.

The body is made of Indonesian Mahogany, the neck is a 5-piece Maple/Walnut laminate, and the fingerboard is Indian Laurel.

It features a single original humbucking pickup.

Despite its simple controls, it delivers a clear, responsive sound with ample low-end.

It features 24 frets, including stainless steel frets.

The laminated neck also offers high rigidity and excellent stability.

The extremely lightweight body (approximately 2.8kg) combined with the 30-inch scale makes it incredibly easy to handle.

The headless design provides excellent weight balance, resulting in comfortable playing even during long sessions.

While incorporating modern features throughout, this guitar achieves a balance between ease of use and high craftsmanship.

This model is recommended even for those who are new to headless basses.

Controls: Volume, Tone
Nut Width: 37.5mm
Bridge String Spacing: 20.0mm
Scale Length: 30 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: INDONESIA

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

item.description:
$1,364 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including James Jamerson, Bobby Vega, and Michael League. (Titles omitted)

The Precision Bass was born in 1951 as the world's first fretted solid-body electric bass, and for over 70 years since then, it has remained the standard bass, supported by players worldwide.

This model is the Classic Series '50s Precision Bass Lacquer, modeled after the late 1950s Precision Bass.

It features vintage-like wood materials, with an alder body and a maple neck and fingerboard.

The finish is lacquer.

The lacquer finish is appealing because it gains character and patina with use.

It's equipped with original Vintage-Style Split Single-Coil pickups.

The thick, solid low end and open, clear midrange are pleasing, delivering the quintessential Precision Bass sound.

Its simple design allows it to faithfully express the nuances of your playing, making it suitable for R&B, rock, pop, and all other genres.

Fret life is approximately 70%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some minor scratches and small dents.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Volume, Tone
Nut width:
Bridge string spacing: 20.0mm
Scale: 34 Inch

item.title: 【used】Sadowsky / MX22 WL4 CAR/R 2025 4.115kg ##A001398-25【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,270
item.description:
$1,270 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)

This model is a Will Lee signature model from the Metroline Express series.

The body is made of Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro).

The lightweight and well-balanced wood construction provides both ease of handling and a natural resonance.

The 22-fret specification is another feature unique to the Will Lee model.

The satin-finish roasted maple neck has a smooth feel, ensuring comfortable playability even during long playing sessions.

Both the pickups and preamp are Sadowsky originals.

In addition to the active sound with a 2-band EQ, it features a practical push/pull switch to passive mode.

The preamp is designed using technology inherited from higher-end models, delivering clear and powerful lows and rich highs.

This guitar delivers the tight, fast-attacking sound characteristic of Sadowsky, accommodating a wide range of playing styles from slap to fingerstyle.

The body balance, sound, and playability are all at a high level, making it a truly exceptional instrument worthy of bearing the name of Will Lee.

The frets have approximately 80% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

There are some minor scratches on the body and paintwork. There is some paint chipping at the neck joint.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were found.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Bass (Push-Pull / Active-Passive), Treble
Nut Width: 37.8mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2025
Country of Manufacture: CHINA

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you purchase an instrument, you will receive a 10% discount on the sales price when you use our trade-in service. (Consignment items are excluded. This discount applies only if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the sales price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: DANELECTRO LONGHORN BASS Copper Burst [20397] (07/10) — $680.61
item.description:
$680.61 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A model with a one-of-a-kind body shape has arrived as a used item!

Founded in 1947, Danelectro began manufacturing electric guitars in the mid-1950s.
is a brand known for producing unique, one-of-a-kind models?whether guitars or basses?that stand out from the crowd.

As the name suggests, this instrument is the gLONGHORN BASS,h characterized by its distinctive shape with both horns elongated and tapered.
In recent years, this model has become well-known through its use by Victoria De Angelis, the bassist for M Neskin.

Despite its short scale and extremely lightweight design at 2.66 kg, the lipstick-shaped gLipstickh pickup delivers a powerful sound.

Although it does not interfere with playing, the neck is in poor condition.
Due to the design of the truss rod adjustment mechanism, adjusting the truss rod is difficult.

Please be aware of this in advance.

There are some minor scratches and some tarnish on the metal parts.
There is what appears to be a sticker mark on the side of the headstock.
The strap pin has been moved to the horn section on the 4th string side.

item.title: Ibanez GSR200 GIO Soundgear 4-String Bass, Black - Used — $199
item.description:
$199 · Very Good · Mill River Music & Guitar Shop

Ibanez GSR200 GIO Soundgear 4-String Bass, Black - Used

The Ibanez GSR200 is one of the most popular entry-level basses on the market for good reason — slim, lightweight, and genuinely comfortable to play, with a P/J pickup configuration and the active PHAT-II bass boost giving you a solid range of tones for the price. The fast maple neck and compact Soundgear body make it an easy instrument to spend a lot of time on, and at 7 pounds 5 ounces this one is notably light. A great player at a great price.
Cosmetically it shows light scratches on the body and a more noticeable scratch on the back consistent with buckle rash, plus a few minor nicks and dents on the neck. Nothing structural, and nothing that affects playability. See photos for detail.
Condition is Very Good.

Make: Ibanez
Model: GSR200 GIO Soundgear
Type: 4-string solid body electric bass
Finish: Black
Body: Poplar
Neck: Maple, bolt-on, 1-piece
Fingerboard: Jatoba
Scale length: 34 inches
Nut width: 41mm
Frets: 22, medium
Pickups: Dynamix P (neck), Dynamix J (bridge)
Controls: Volume x2, tone, PHAT-II active bass boost
Bridge: B10 (19mm string spacing)
Hardware: Chrome
Weight: 7 lbs 5 oz
Condition: Very Good
Known issues: Buckle rash on back, minor nicks and dents on neck
Modifications: None
Includes: Nothing

Ships carefully packed and insured for full value.
International buyers are welcome — please contact us for a shipping quote before purchasing.
About Mill River Music
Mill River Music is an independent, human-powered music shop located in downtown Northampton, MA, run by a team of players, enthusiasts, and certified repair technicians. Every piece of used gear we sell is personally inspected, tested, and set up before it ships — we don't list it until we'd be happy to play it ourselves.
As Martin and Taylor certified techs, we hold our guitar work to a high standard, and we're committed to full transparency about the condition of everything we sell. No surprises.
When you buy from Mill River, you're supporting an independent local business that genuinely cares — about gear, about players, and about doing things right. We're here every step of the way. Reach out anytime.

item.title: Fender JB62 Jazz Bass | 1995 Made in Japan - Black Gloss — $941
item.description:
$941 · Very Good · Guitar Garage

1995 Fender JB62 Jazz Bass in Black. Basswood body. Maple neck. Rosewood slab fingerboard. 7.25" radius. 34" scale. 20 frets. Dual JB-Hotrod single-coils. Passive electronics. 3.6kg. Gig bag included.

The JB62 is a reissue of the 1962 Jazz Bass, built at the Fujigen factory during the N-serial era (1993-1994). The rosewood fingerboard has no fretwear. The black gloss finish shows light wear consistent with age.

Test it for yourself at our Sydney shop, or we can ship it safely to your door anywhere in Australia. Contact us for a shipping quote.

In stock at Guitar Garage Sydney.

Key Features

Year: 1995
Made in: Japan (Fujigen factory)
Serial: N041045
Model: JB62 (1962 Jazz Bass Reissue)
Finish: Black (gloss polyester)
Body: Basswood
Neck: Maple, slim 60s Jazz oval C profile
Fingerboard: Rosewood slab
Radius: 7.25"
Scale: 34"
Nut Width: 38.1mm
Frets: 20 vintage-style (no wear)
Pickups: JB-Hotrod single-coils
Controls: Volume 1 (neck), Volume 2 (bridge), Master Tone
Bridge: Spiral/threaded barrel saddle
Tuners: Reverse-gear open
Pickguard: 3-ply white/black/white, 11-hole
Hardware: Chrome
Weight: 3.6kg
Case: Gig bag
Condition: Excellent

Sound and Tone

JB-Hotrod ceramic pickups deliver punchy, mid-forward Jazz Bass growl. Rosewood fingerboard adds warmth. Passive electronics provide simple, effective tone shaping.

Feel and Playability

Slim oval C maple neck. 7.25" radius rosewood slab board. No fretwear. Lightweight 3.6kg.

Condition

Excellent. Rosewood fingerboard shows no fretwear. Black body shows light scratching and one small paint chip on the right side of the chrome control panel. Pickups show slight vintage oxidation on pole pieces but are fully functional, quiet and punchy.

item.title: Spector Euro5LX 5-String Bass Poplar Burl Bartolini NB14359 — $2,290
item.description:
$2,290 · Excellent · Fret Nation

Pre-Owned Spector EURO 5 LX - Poplar Burl Natural

Model Number: EURO5LXPOPB
Serial Number: NB14359
Weight: 9.72 Pounds

This is a beautiful pre-owned Spector Euro5LX in Poplar Burl.  The bass does not have any finish dings scratches or cracks that we can find.  The bass is all original with a fresh set of Spector Nickel Plated Steel strings.
The bass does not include a case.  We will ship securely using a generic bag or case.  


Any questions at all please do not hesitate to ask! 

General information about this model:

Spector Euro5 LX Neck-Thru Poplar Burl 5-String Bass 35" Scale
The Spector Euro5 LX Electric Bass is an extremely comfortable bass to play with its fully carved European alder body.  This Euro5 LX has a beautiful Poplar Burl top with a walnut accent strip.  This Spector Euro5LX bass has a 3-Piece maple neck has a thru-body construction with a 24 fret rosewood fingerboard with Spector's signature Crown inlays.  The Spector Euro5 LX has a lightweight zinc brass alloy bridge and nut, Passive custom-wound Bartolini pickups and Spector's coveted Tone Pump active tone circuit.  Gold hardware and a beautiful natural high gloss finish makes this instrument look as stunning as it sounds.

Spector Euro5 LX Neck-Thru Specifications:

Neck:

-- Wood: 3 piece Maple w/ Graphite Rods: Neck Thru construction
-- Fingerboard: Rosewood, Spector Mother of Pearl Crown Inlays
-- Number of Frets: 24
-- Scale Length: 35"
-- Radius: 16"
-- Width at Nut: 1.64"

Body:

-- Spector Original NS curved and carved body shape
-- Poplar Burl Top over European Alder Body with Walnut veneer.  
-- Natural High-Gloss Finish

Hardware:

-- Gold Hardware
-- Bridge: Spector Zinc Brass alloy locking bridge, with intonation screws
-- String Spacing at Bridge: .75"
-- Strap Buttons: Schaller Locking
-- Brass Nut

Pickups and Electronics:

-- Neck Pickups: Custom Wound Passive Bartolini
-- Bridge Pickup: Custom Wound Passive Bartolini
-- Active Tone Controls: Spector TonePump
-- Boost Only: + 18dB Bass, + 18dB Treble
-- Controls: Bridge Pickup Volume, Neck Pickup Volume, Treble Boost, Bass Boost
This is a pre-owned instrument and is exempt from our new instrument return policy.  FretNation guarantees the instrument to be delivered in fully working condition and as described.

item.title: Sadowsky MX24 MS4 - OWT/M - [3.67kg] — $706.30
item.description:
$706.30 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Ochanomizu

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

This includes situations such as high customs duties, shipping delays, product colors differing from expectations, items being heavy, difficult to play ability.





The Sadowsky Metro Express series—reborn while honoring tradition—has arrived.

Thanks to a collaboration with Warwick, it has undergone subtle refinements and returned with even greater practicality.


Of course, the compact “Dinky” body size unique to Sadowsky models remains intact.

This compact size makes it easy to hold and lightweight, resulting in exceptional playability.



It features the Sadowsky Just-A-Nut III, which allows you to adjust the nut height with a set screw.

This system is also familiar from Warwick models, and once you realize how convenient it is to freely adjust the nut height, you’ll want this feature on all your basses.


The model we’ve just received is a hidden gem from Sadowsky: the soapbar-style model.

You can enjoy a refined, high-end sound at the Metro Express’s affordable price point.


Get that iconic Sadowsky sound you all know and love at this price.

Don’t miss this opportunity.

Fingerboard: Roasted Maple fingerboard

item.description:
$1,656.59 · Very Good · Kurosawa Music Ikebukuro

-----

*Caution!!

To prevent fraudulent purchases, we do not accept PayPal guest checkout.

Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

-----

Welcome to

Kurosawa Ikebukuro! We can ship anywhere all over the world!!

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details. Also, please carefully read our

shop policy below the guitar description!

For Paypal payments, please register your address in your Paypal account in advance.

------------------------------------------

A special Bacchus model produced in a limited run of 10 to commemorate the brand’s 30th anniversary in 2024!

This mystical bass features “Jindai-ki”—wood that has remained buried underground for over 1,000 years without decaying!


The sound is distinct from that of alder or ash; the high frequencies seem significantly subdued,

so I think this bass is better suited for players who want a solid, resilient tone when fingerpicking or playing with a pick, rather than for aggressive slapping.



・“Jindai-ki” Ash 2-Piece Body


Wood that has remained buried underground for over 1,000 years without decaying is reverently referred to as “Jindai-ki.”

To commemorate the 30th anniversary of the Bacchus brand, this model lavishly features rare Kamidoki ash wood for its two-piece body.

You can fully savor the unique tone of Kamidoki, along with its rich history.



・Thermo-Maple Neck


“Thermo-Wood” is a processing method in which wood is heated to high temperatures through a special heat treatment to evaporate moisture and oils, creating a condition similar to that of vintage wood that has aged over many years. This not only enhances the neck’s strength but also produces a mature, aged sound reminiscent of a guitar that has been played for many years.



・Ziricote Fingerboard


The fingerboard is crafted from ziricote, a wood known for its rich, deep grain pattern, responsive low end, and exceptional hardness and high specific gravity. This specification is exclusive to this special model and is not found in the regular lineup.


[Spec]


Body：Jindai Tamo2P
Neck：Thermo Maple
Fingerboard：Ziricote
Frets：SANKO SBB214
Nut：Bone
Machineheads：GOTOH GB11W
Bridge：GOTOH 404SJ-4
Pickups：Bacchus JB ALVⅡ
Controls：2Vol,1Tone (Pull up Turbo SW)
Scale：864mm(34inch)
Width at Nut：38mm
Radius：310R
Finish：Top Lacquer




[Condition]


Appearance: No noticeable scratches; while some parts show signs of wear, it is in excellent condition.


Neck: Currently adjusted to be straight. There is no excessive bow or extreme twist.


Truss Rod: Adjustable in both directions; functions properly.


Frets: There are slight string marks, but no muting or other issues.



Accessories: Gig bag

item.title: Flight Mini Bass Lite - Sea Foam Green — $175
item.description:
$175 · Excellent · Griffmusic

Cool little bass with a big thump. A great grab and go bass to take to a jam or on a trip. Fun to have around the house because it is not big and bulky. Easy to play on the couch. Still has plastic on the pick guard and pick ups.

Cont USA only.

item.title: B.C. Rich N.J. Mockingbird. 1984 - Natural walnut and maple. Neck through body. — $1,850
item.description:
$1,850 · Very Good · Church of Guitars

This is a very early B.C. Rich N.J. Mockingbird. Neck through body construction. Very rare.
I’ve never seen one with the serial number on the back of the body and it also came with a metal control plate cavity cover. My friends that work at BC Rich are telling me that it’s most likely a prototype in natural satin finish walnut in maple. This bass is in very good condition but the strap buttons at one time were moved not for a left-handed player, but to try to prevent neck dive.

The pick-ups could use new foam underneath and one of the knobs is not matching. It sounds good and it plays really well. It has a Leo Quan badass bridge and sustains very well. I do not have a hard shell case for it but I will ship it in a gig bag and it will be packaged very well, insured and protected.

I will only ship in the United States.

Thank you for looking.

item.title: Moollon J5 2013 - 3-Tone Sunburst — $3,850
item.description:
$3,850 · Good · Prado’s Music Garage

Up for sale is my 2013 Moollon J5 in a beautiful 3-Tone Sunburst finish. If you’re looking at this ad, you probably already know what these basses are all about. This one has the classic vintage Moollon tone—big, open, woody, and incredibly responsive.

One of my favorite features is the original brown tortoise pickguard, which Moollon no longer offers. It really complements the vintage sunburst and gives it a classic early-’60s Jazz Bass aesthetic.

The bass was recently professionally set up, including a full fret level, crown, and polish, so it plays exceptionally well with a low, comfortable action up and down the neck. No dead spots or surprises—it’s ready to gig or record immediately.

Specs

  • 2013 Moollon J5
  • 3-Tone Sunburst
  • Rosewood fingerboard
  • Rare/discontinued brown tortoise pickguard
  • 9.3 lbs
  • Fresh professional setup
  • Recently leveled, crowned, and polished frets
  • Includes hard shell case

Condition

I’d rate it as Good / Very Good overall. It has honest player wear throughout, including finish chips and edge wear that are shown in the photos. Nothing affects playability whatsoever—just the kind of natural wear you’d expect from a well-loved instrument that’s been played instead of sitting in a closet.

The neck is straight, truss rod functions as it should, electronics are quiet, and everything works exactly as intended.

Tone

If you’ve never owned a Moollon, they’re among the closest modern recreations of an exceptional vintage Jazz Bass I’ve played. Huge fundamental, articulate low B, sweet mids, and plenty of growl when you dig in. It sits in a mix effortlessly.

item.title: 2008 Fender American Standard Precision Bass – With OHSC and Case Candy! — $1,600
item.description:
$1,600 · Excellent · L.A. Vintage Gear

A modern take on one of the most iconic basses ever built, this 2008 Fender American Standard Precision Bass delivers the legendary punch, clarity, and reliability that has made the P Bass the foundation of countless recordings and live performances. This bass is a real closet classic, having been stored in it's case for nearly 20 years! The plastic is still on the tuning machines! There is one small blemish on the back of the body near the bottom horn (shown in photos).


Finished in classic Black with a matching black pickguard and a one-piece maple neck, this bass has a timeless, understated look that never goes out of style. The American Standard series introduced several thoughtful upgrades over previous generations, including Fender's HiMass bridge for increased sustain and resonance, lightweight vintage-style tuners, a synthetic bone nut, and Posiflex graphite reinforcement rods for exceptional neck stability.


The American Standard Alnico V split-coil pickup provides everything players expect from a great Precision Bass: deep, authoritative lows, focused mids, and enough articulation to sit perfectly in any mix. From classic rock and Motown to punk, country, blues, and modern pop, this bass delivers the unmistakable P Bass sound that has defined electric bass for more than six decades.

A true American-made workhorse, the 2008 American Standard Precision Bass combines vintage-inspired tone with modern playability and dependable performance. Whether you're looking for your first serious P Bass or adding another classic to your collection, this USA-built Fender is ready for the studio, stage, or everyday playing.

Specs:

  • Body Wood: Alder
  • Body Shape: Precision Bass
  • Finish: Gloss Urethane
  • Neck Wood: Maple
  • Neck Shape: Modern "C"
  • Neck Finish: Satin Urethane (Back), Gloss Urethane (Front)
  • Scale Length: 34" (864 mm)
  • Fingerboard: Maple
  • Radius: 9.5" (241 mm)
  • Frets: 20 Medium Jumbo
  • Nut Material: Synthetic Bone
  • Nut Width: 1.625" (41.3 mm)
  • Pickup: American Standard Alnico V Split Single-Coil Precision Bass Pickup
  • Bridge: 4-Saddle HiMass™ Vintage Bridge (String-Through-Body or Top-Load)
  • Weight: 8.7 lbs

Medium Jumbo frets paired with a 9.5" radius support fast, accurate fretting, while the neck depth progression from 0.820" at the first fret to 0.920" at the 12th fret creates a contoured feel that supports both fingerstyle and pick playing.

Tone & Electronics

Two G&L JB Alnico V single-coil pickups deliver classic J-bass character with excellent articulation and clarity. The dual volume and single tone control setup allows intuitive blending and shaping of the pickup mix, while the Saddle-Lock bridge ensures rock-solid intonation and sustain.

Key Features

The Schecter custom bass bridge with string-through design maximizes sustain and tonal clarity while the active electronics run from a standard 9-volt clip-in battery compartment.

Key Features
  • 34" scale length with thin "C" neck profile (0.787" at 1st fret, 0.866" at 12th fret)
  • Three-piece maple neck with carbon fiber reinforcement rods
  • Mahogany body with flamed maple top and black 1-ply binding
  • 24 X-Jumbo frets on ebony fretboard with 16" radius
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut, 1.496" width
  • EMG 35DC bridge pickup and EMG 35P4 neck pickup
  • Volume/Blend control with 2-band EMG EQ
  • Schecter custom bass bridge with string-through design
  • Schecter tuners with 2-way adjustable truss rod and heel access
  • Black hardware throughout with metal knurled knobs and set screws
  • Right-handed configuration with offset/reverse Roman numeral inlays
  • Set-neck construction with ultra access to upper frets

This C-4 GT combines professional-grade electronics, refined playability, and standout aesthetics at B-stock pricing—a solid choice for players seeking reliable performance without compromise.

This specific B-Stock unit features minor cosmetic imperfections from standard warehouse handling and processing. These marks are purely superficial and have absolutely zero impact on the playability, tone, or structural integrity of the guitar. Acquire this premium Schecter C-4 GT Bass at a significant B-Stock discount while receiving the full manufacturer warranty and the same exceptional craftsmanship as new inventory.

item.title: El Degas EL 780 ca.1978 Natural, Made in Japan — $1,100
item.description:
$1,100 · Very Good · Bass Seller

Vintage 1970s El Degas Neck-Through Alembic-Style Bass – Made in Japan – Original Case, strap and cable. Was a gift, many years ago, never played it, but someone should. Cloth-braided shielded wire, commonly used on higher-quality Japanese instruments.

  • Foil shielding in the cavity (a nice touch for reducing noise).
  • The green film capacitors are consistent with electronics from the late 1970s/early 1980s.

From my research...

"The body shape, moon inlays, and center laminates don't exactly match the common El Degas catalogs. That raises the possibility that this instrument was built by one of the better Japanese factories and sold under the El Degas name for the Canadian market. During the late 1970s, several distributors sourced instruments from factories like Matsumoku, Kasuga, FujiGen, and others, sometimes with subtle variations between runs."
